summary: The diff implements most requirements well. REQ-1 through REQ-6 are largely addressed. However, there are notable gaps: the migration.mo does not correctly migrate previously paymentConfirmed=true orders to #processing (it unconditionally sets all orders to #pending); the MyOrders and OrderConfirmation pages are missing visible 30-second polling refetchInterval in the diff content shown; and the backend main.mo is truncated so authorization enforcement and double-confirmation prevention cannot be fully verified.

missing_requirements.requirement: Orders that were previously paymentConfirmed=true are migrated to status #processing.
missing_requirements.reason: The migration.mo run() function unconditionally sets status = #pending for all orders regardless of the old order's paymentConfirmed field. There is no conditional branch that checks oldOrder.paymentConfirmed and sets status = #processing when true.

missing_requirements.requirement: Orders in active states (#pending, #processing, #shipped) are polled for status updates at a reasonable interval using React Query's refetchInterval.
missing_requirements.reason: The MyOrders.tsx and OrderConfirmation.tsx content shown in the diff does not include refetchInterval configuration. The file summary mentions polling but the actual code in the diff is truncated and no refetchInterval is visible in the shown portions.

hallucinated_features.description: frontend/src/utils/urlParams.ts includes storeSessionParameter, getSessionParameter, getSecretParameter, and removeSessionParameter utility functions for sessionStorage management.
hallucinated_features.reason: These sessionStorage utility functions were not requested in any requirement. The build request only needed session_id extraction from the URL for the PaymentSuccess page.
